reasoning: The stock has faced recent downward momentum, falling closely from $60.34 on May 14 to $56.03 on May 23. The statistical tool estimate for the near-term (June 1) provides a combined estimate of p=0.41 that the price will remain above the threshold, primarily due to the current linear downtrend. This serves as our primary anchor for the near-term probability. Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon and drive probabilities closer to 0.5 for more distant dates, the estimates are tapered gradually from 0.41 to 0.49 over the four dates. Although there are strong fundamental factors, such as a recently increased quarterly dividend of $0.30 and a massive $2.5B share buyback program that might drive the stock up over longer horizons, the explicit constraint asks for distance-to-0.5 to decrease over time. The forecast, therefore, assigns the highest confidence (furthest from 0.5) to the short term due to observable momentum, while treating the long term as largely uncertain (closest to 0.5).
